response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the role's hard requirements are largely unmet: no native-level Japanese proficiency (a strict requirement), no evidence of Linux kernel investigation or source modification, and only a brief stint (~6 months at National Instruments) of C/C++ work versus the strong C-for-UNIX proficiency required. There is no demonstrated experience with embedded LAN-switch products, L2/L3 networking internals, or security technologies like MACsec, 802.1X, or tamper detection. Despite solid general engineering craftsmanship, the profile does not align with this embedded networking security position.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) and open-source contributions
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux experience
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese
No Linux kernel analysis/modification experience or embedded networking security background (MACsec, 802.1X, switch chips)
C programming experience is minimal and dated; career trajectory is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Strong general engineering fundamentals and testing culture
